root_text.an3.28:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabhaggato vā parisaggato vā ñātimajjhagato vā pūgamajjhagato vā rājakulamajjhagato vā abhinīto sakkhipuṭṭho: ‘ehambho purisa, yaṁ jānāsi taṁ vadehī’ti. So ajānaṁ vā āha: ‘jānāmī’ti, jānaṁ vā āha: ‘na jānāmī’ti, apassaṁ vā āha: ‘passāmī’ti, passaṁ vā āha: ‘na passāmī’ti; iti attahetu vā parahetu vā āmisakiñcikkhahetu vā sampajānamusā bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an3.28:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabhaggato vā parisaggato vā ñātimajjhagato vā pūgamajjhagato vā rājakulamajjhagato vā abhinīto sakkhipuṭṭho: ‘ehambho purisa, yaṁ pajānāsi taṁ vadehī’ti, so ajānaṁ vā āha: ‘na jānāmī’ti, jānaṁ vā āha: ‘jānāmī’ti, apassaṁ vā āha: ‘na passāmī’ti, passaṁ vā āha: ‘passāmī’ti; iti attahetu vā parahetu vā āmisakiñcikkhahetu vā na sampajānamusā bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an3.28:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo pharusaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti;
root_text.an3.28:3.3: yā sā vācā nelā kaṇṇasukhā pemanīyā hadayaṅgamā porī bahujanakantā bahujanamanāpā tathārūpiṁ vācaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an3.28:3.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an3.28:1.1: “These three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.28:1.3: One with speech like dung, one with speech like flowers, and one with speech like honey.
translation_text.an3.28:1.4: And who has speech like dung?
translation_text.an3.28:1.5: It’s an individual who is summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my friend, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ So they deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason.
translation_text.an3.28:1.6: This is called an individual with speech like dung.
translation_text.an3.28:2.1: And who has speech like flowers?
translation_text.an3.28:2.2: It’s an individual who is summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my friend, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I see.’ So they don’t deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason.
translation_text.an3.28:2.3: This is called an individual with speech like flowers.
translation_text.an3.28:3.1: And who has speech like honey?
translation_text.an3.28:3.2: It’s an individual who gives up harsh speech.
translation_text.an3.28:3.3: They speak in a way that’s mellow, pleasing to the ear, lovely, going to the heart, polite, likable and agreeable to the people.
translation_text.an3.28:3.4: This is called an individual with speech like honey.
translation_text.an3.28:3.5: These are the three individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an3.28:1.5: sabhaggato vā → sabhāgoto vā (sya-all, km) | parisaggato vā → parisagato vā (si, sya-all, km)

reference_text.an3.28:1.1: csp1ed15.126, csp2ed15.126, ms15A3_138, msdiv28, pts-vp-pli1.128, vri35.151
